page: The Microsoft Azure D4ads_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dadv6 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The server features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with multithreading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, translating to 4.0 GB per core, and includes 236 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities. The virtualization layer is managed via Microsoft Hyper-V.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU testing placing in the top 10% and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%. Single-threaded text processing and bzip3 decompression also demonstrate strong performance. However, multi-threaded scaling and memory bandwidth present notable tradeoffs. Memory bandwidth for read-only operations, both cached small blocks and potentially uncached large blocks, falls into the bottom 25%. Similarly, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and software compilation with Clang are positioned in the lower 25% tier.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, the D4ads_v6 offers standard resource density for general-purpose workloads. It is well-suited for static web serving, single-threaded applications, and lightweight Redis deployments, where its average multi-core and transactional performance are sufficient. However, due to weak database operations benchmarks and poor prompt processing speeds for medium-sized language models, it is less suitable for heavy database hosting or complex AI inference tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure D4ads_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dadv6 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 x86_64 processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz, 16.0 GB of system memory, and 236 GB of local storage. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong single-threaded text processing. However, it exhibits lower performance in memory read bandwidth, multi-threaded decompression, and database operations. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides balanced resource density, making it a viable option for static web serving, lightweight caching, and general-purpose applications. It is less optimal for memory-intensive database workloads or large-scale multi-threaded compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4ads_v6 is a general-purpose VM featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 236 GB local storage. It offers strong single-core performance but exhibits lower memory read bandwidth.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4ads_v6 is a general-purpose VM with 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 236 GB local storage, managed via Hyper-V.
tagline: A general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing, local storage, and strong single-core performance for balanced workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs.
Equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM and 236 GB of local storage.
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU and strong single-threaded decompression performance.
Exhibits lower performance in memory read bandwidth and database operations.
Best suited for static web serving, lightweight caching, and general-purpose applications.
